name: Starlette
what_it_does: Lightweight ASGI toolkit providing routing, WebSocket support, background tasks, middleware, and request/response primitives — the foundation underlying FastAPI.
use_cases: Building custom ASGI middleware layers that intercept and transform agent HTTP traffic
Hosting lightweight agent-facing HTTP endpoints without the overhead of a full framework
Serving WebSocket connections for real-time bidirectional agent-to-client communication
Running background tasks (e.g., async job dispatch) that decouple from the HTTP request lifecycle
Writing integration tests for ASGI apps using Starlette's built-in TestClient without spinning up a server
not_for: Teams wanting automatic request validation and OpenAPI docs out of the box (use FastAPI instead)
Legacy WSGI deployments that cannot adopt an async server like uvicorn or hypercorn
Applications needing a batteries-included ORM, admin panel, or full MVC structure
best_when: You need full control over ASGI primitives with minimal abstraction, or you are building a framework layer on top of ASGI.
avoid_when: You want automatic schema validation, dependency injection, and OpenAPI generation without writing boilerplate — use FastAPI.

auth.notes: No built-in auth — developers implement authentication via middleware (e.g., starlette-auth, authlib).

performance.rate_limits: None (library); rate limiting must be implemented via middleware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP exceptions are clean and structured; unhandled exceptions in ASGI scope produce terse 500 responses without detail by default.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No built-in idempotency keys; must be implemented at the application layer.
known_agent_gotchas: Starlette does not validate request bodies automatically — agents that skip explicit parsing can silently receive None for missing fields
Background tasks run after the response is sent; if the ASGI server shuts down mid-task, the task is lost with no acknowledgment
WebSocket connections do not auto-reconnect — agents relying on long-lived sockets must implement their own reconnection logic
TestClient uses requests (sync) under the hood; async test patterns require httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport
Middleware ordering matters and is non-obvious — wrapping order is reversed (last added = outermost), which surprises many developers

security_score_components.security_notes: No TLS at library level — handled by ASGI server (uvicorn). No built-in CSRF or auth; developers must add these. Minimal dependency footprint.
